mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show a non-positive Spearman correlation with 20-day S&P 500 forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The effective federal funds rate crossing above 0.2 percent signals the conclusion of zero-interest-rate policy and the onset of monetary normalization, which tightens financial conditions and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.2%, positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer prices and deceleration in industrial production forces an adverse repricing of real discount rates and corporate margins, so the interaction of rising CPIAUCSL and falling INDPRO predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of CPIAUCSL and INDPRO does not exhibit a statistically negative relationship with 60-day S&P 500 returns (regression t-stat > -1.5) over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, accelerating consumer price inflation causes the market to revise terminal rate expectations upward faster than nominal earnings growth, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Within tightening cycles, month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect expanding manufacturing and utilities output, which supports near-term positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through higher realized earnings surprises.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of retail and food services sales below its trailing three-month average indicates weakening consumer demand momentum, which drags down revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS crosses below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: When RSAFS crosses below its 3-month moving average, positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently rising unemployment and falling housing starts generate a joint recession probability signal that depresses risk appetite and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of UNRATE increasing month-over-month and HOUST decreasing month-over-month does not produce negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real chained GDP signals broad-based aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate sales and profits, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly changes in GDPC1 fail to show a positive rank correlation with 60-day S&P 500 forward returns (Spearman rho < 0.03) over any 5-year rolling window.
